text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - tech and semis holding up despite AI policy noise from Washington. The real driver is the inflation narrative softening (headline 10 says fears overblown), which keeps rate-cut hopes alive. That's fuel for your kind of names. Warsh inflation hawk talk (headline 2) is the counterweight - watch if that gains traction.

Your holdings: PTNM is bleeding at -15.25% and close to its $9.50 stop - on margin that pain is real, this needs watching first. SOXL is a 3x leveraged ETF, currently +3.51% but already well above your $200 stop - decay accelerates in choppy tape so keep that stop active. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history - margin amplifies any sudden drop here.

Watch: Whether Warsh's inflation hawkishness moves the bond market (yields rising = headwind for tech multiples). Also watch SMH at the $637-634 zone - semis are your core exposure.

Bottom line: Protect PTNM and SOXL first - new trades can wait until those are managed.
plays: PTNM: Stop is $9.50 - price is $10.39, only 8.6% away. On margin, decide now whether to tighten or exit; a close under $9.50 means out, no exceptions.
SOXL: Still holding +3.51% but 3x decay is real. Scanner shows accumulate zone $238-234 if it pulls back; trail your stop up toward $220-225 to lock some gains.
No new buys today - CRWD setup (scanner entry $740-744, stop $731) is the cleanest Grade A pattern but you already own it at +4.95%; no need to add unless it dips cleanly into that zone.
